codeReview.summary: Core notification gateway pieces are implemented and targeted tests pass, but two correctness gaps block acceptance: webhook routing bypasses the IntegrationAdapter path entirely, and integration status can be persisted as active before connect succeeds.

issues.description: Webhook ingestion does not use the WebhookIntegration adapter/registry path. The route constructs NotificationEvent directly and writes to store/eventBus, so webhook adapters loaded via IntegrationRegistry are not actually involved, violating the feature requirement that webhook integration implements and handles events via IntegrationAdapter.

IntegrationRegistry persists config with status 'active' before adapter.connect() completes. If connect throws, DB state incorrectly marks integration active despite failed startup, creating stale status and inconsistent behavior.

Comment says echo integration is inactive by default, but code immediately loads and starts echo-default integration at startup. This is behavior/comment mismatch and can generate unrequested synthetic traffic.
Webhook endpoint bypasses WebhookIntegration adapter and registry flow.
issues.evidence: src/api/admin.ts:78-99 creates NotificationEvent directly and calls store.insert/eventBus.emitNotification; src/integrations/builtin/webhook.ts:45 defines handleWebhookPost but it is never invoked from runtime code (only tests).
issues.suggestedFix: Route POST /webhooks/:integrationId through the loaded webhook adapter instance from IntegrationRegistry (or remove unused adapter and align feature scope). Ensure adapter.onEvent pipeline is the authoritative path for webhook events.

issues.description: Integration is marked active in DB before successful connect, causing incorrect persisted state on failures.
issues.evidence: src/integrations/registry.ts:76-85 calls storeIntegrationConfig(...,'active') before await adapter.connect(config).
issues.suggestedFix: Persist as inactive/pending first, call connect, then update status to active only after successful connection (or wrap in transaction with rollback/update on failure).

issues.description: Echo integration startup behavior contradicts inline comment about being inactive by default.
issues.evidence: src/main.ts:66 comment says 'inactive by default' but line 68 immediately calls loadIntegration('echo-default', 'echo', ...).
issues.suggestedFix: Either remove auto-start or update comment/feature docs to match intended default behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata references skillName 'worker', but .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md is missing and the handoff records a deviation because 'worker' skill was not found.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: This causes repeated process deviations and inconsistent execution standards during worker runs; mission metadata or available skills should be aligned.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Expected architecture reference file .factory/library/architecture.md is absent in this repository.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Reviewers/workers are instructed to validate against architecture boundaries, but lack a canonical architecture document to reference.

commandsRun.observation: Feature metadata, expected behavior, and fulfills assertions confirmed.
Reviewed commit scope: 15 files, 1665 insertions for f2 implementation.
Transcript skeleton for assigned worker session reviewed.
Targeted tests pass (2 suites, 18 tests), but they do not cover runtime wiring between admin webhook route and WebhookIntegration adapter registry usage.
Confirmed handleWebhookPost exists only in adapter/tests and is not called by admin runtime path.

summary: Verdict: fail. The feature includes most required components, but webhook processing bypasses the integration adapter system and integration status persistence can become incorrect when connect fails, both of which are blocking correctness issues.
